page: Microsoft Azure M64ms is an x86_64 memory-intensive server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8280L processors. It features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 38 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. The server is equipped with 1792.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 28.0 GB per core, and includes 2097 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff. Single-core performance is weak, as evidenced by low scores in stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests, alongside poor single-threaded compression and decompression rates. However, multi-core capabilities are average, with strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance. Memory subsystem benchmarks show strong uncached large-block read bandwidth at 196.25 GB/sec, while cached small-block reads and large-block writes perform at average levels.

Given its massive memory footprint and average multi-core processing capabilities, the M64ms offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-intensive tasks. The high resource density of RAM makes it suitable for large in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and heavy database operations, as reflected in its average Redis SET and Passmark database benchmark results. It is less optimal for single-threaded applications or workloads requiring high-speed LLM text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure M64ms is a memory-optimized x86_64 server designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs utilizing Intel Xeon 8280L processors running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 1792.0 GB of RAM and 2097 GB of local storage. While single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression benchmarks are weak, the server demonstrates strong uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 196.25 GB/sec and average multi-core processing capabilities. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 28.0 GB per core makes this server qualitatively cost-efficient for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and data-intensive enterprise applications, whereas it is less suited for compute-bound or single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64ms is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 1792 GB of RAM, and 2097 GB of local storage, designed for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64ms is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 1792 GB of RAM, and 2097 GB of local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized server featuring massive RAM density and local storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8280L processors at 2.7 GHz
1792 GB of system memory providing 28 GB per core
2097 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 196.25 GB/sec
Best suited for large in-memory databases, caching, and enterprise applications
